page: Microsoft Azure FX16-8ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a dedicated clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled. It features a massive memory capacity of 336.0 GB, translating to 42.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The CPU is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX16-8ms_v2 demonstrates strong single-core performance. It achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2338.0 and a text processing score of 2975.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong results, reaching 34.77 GB/sec and 14.31 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations score in the average range, with a Passmark memory score of 2304.48 and Redis SET operations performing at 12.5 million operations per second.

This server does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external premium storage options. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX16-8ms_v2 represents a highly specialized resource profile, offering an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio alongside high-frequency processing. This makes it a premium choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel core scaling but demand high per-core memory capacity and fast single-threaded execution.

The hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive applications, large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring significant memory footprints per active thread. It is also suitable for single-threaded data processing tasks, text processing, and lightweight LLM inference workloads where memory capacity is the primary constraint.
description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server from the FXmsv2 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores and a substantial 336.0 GB of RAM, yielding 42.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and AMX instructions. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it delivers top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2338.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. Multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average range. This server is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring extreme memory density per core rather than massive parallelization. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 8 vCPUs, and 336 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 336 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive server featuring extreme RAM-to-core density and top-tier single-core performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
Extremely high memory capacity of 336.0 GB (42.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
No local storage bundled; premium storage capable
Optimized for in-memory databases and memory-intensive applications
